title: XR-1: Towards Versatile Vision-Language-Action Models via Learning Unified Vision-Motion Representations
summary: Recent progress in large-scale robotic datasets and vision-language models (VLMs) has advanced research on vision-language-action (VLA) models. However, existing VLA models still face two fundamental challenges: (i) producing precise low-level actions from high-dimensional observations, (ii) bridging domain gaps across heterogeneous data sources, including diverse robot embodiments and human demonstrations. Existing methods often encode latent variables from either visual dynamics or robotic actions to guide policy learning, but they fail to fully exploit the complementary multi-modal knowledge present in large-scale, heterogeneous datasets. In this work, we present X Robotic Model 1 (XR-1), a novel framework for versatile and scalable VLA learning across diverse robots, tasks, and environments. XR-1 introduces the \emph{Unified Vision-Motion Codes (UVMC)}, a discrete latent representation learned via a dual-branch VQ-VAE that jointly encodes visual dynamics and robotic motion. UVMC addresses these challenges by (i) serving as an intermediate representation between the observations and actions, and (ii) aligning multimodal dynamic information from heterogeneous data sources to capture complementary knowledge. To effectively exploit UVMC, we propose a three-stage training paradigm: (i) self-supervised UVMC learning, (ii) UVMC-guided pretraining on large-scale cross-embodiment robotic datasets, and (iii) task-specific post-training. We validate XR-1 through extensive real-world experiments with more than 14,000 rollouts on six different robot embodiments, spanning over 120 diverse manipulation tasks. XR-1 consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines such as $π_{0.5}$, $π_0$, RDT, UniVLA, and GR00T-N1.5 while demonstrating strong generalization to novel objects, background variations, distractors, and illumination changes. Our project is at https://xr-1-vla.github.io/.
tldr: The paper introduces XR-1, a Vision-Language-Action model that utilizes Unified Vision-Motion Codes (UVMC) learned via a dual-branch VQ-VAE to address challenges in robotic control across diverse embodiments and tasks. XR-1 demonstrates strong performance and generalization in real-world robotic manipulation experiments.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 XR-1，一个视觉-语言-动作模型，它利用通过双分支 VQ-VAE 学习的统一视觉-运动代码 (UVMC) 来解决不同机器人和任务中的控制挑战。XR-1 在真实机器人操作实验中表现出强大的性能和泛化能力。
summary_zh: 大型机器人数据集和视觉语言模型（VLM）的最新进展推动了视觉语言动作（VLA）模型的研究。然而，现有的VLA模型仍然面临两个根本性的挑战：（i）从高维观测中生成精确的底层动作；（ii）弥合跨异构数据源的领域差距，包括不同的机器人形态和人类演示。现有方法通常编码来自视觉动态或机器人动作的潜在变量来指导策略学习，但它们未能充分利用大规模异构数据集中存在的互补性多模态知识。在这项工作中，我们提出了X Robotic Model 1 (XR-1)，这是一个用于跨不同机器人、任务和环境进行通用且可扩展VLA学习的新颖框架。XR-1引入了\emph{统一视觉-运动码（UVMC）}，这是一种通过双分支VQ-VAE学习的离散潜在表示，它联合编码视觉动态和机器人运动。UVMC通过以下方式应对这些挑战：（i）作为观测和动作之间的中间表示；（ii）对齐来自异构数据源的多模态动态信息以捕获互补知识。为了有效利用UVMC，我们提出了一种三阶段训练范式：（i）自监督UVMC学习；（ii）基于大规模跨形态机器人数据集的UVMC引导预训练；（iii）任务特定的后训练。我们通过超过14,000次真实世界实验验证了XR-1，这些实验在六种不同的机器人形态上进行，跨越120多个不同的操作任务。XR-1始终优于最先进的基线方法，例如$π_{0.5}$、$π_0$、RDT、UniVLA和GR00T-N1.5，同时展示出对新颖物体、背景变化、干扰物和光照变化的强大泛化能力。我们的项目位于https://xr-1-vla.github.io/。

title: From the Laboratory to Real-World Application: Evaluating Zero-Shot Scene Interpretation on Edge Devices for Mobile Robotics
summary: Video Understanding, Scene Interpretation and Commonsense Reasoning are highly challenging tasks enabling the interpretation of visual information, allowing agents to perceive, interact with and make rational decisions in its environment. Large Language Models (LLMs) and Visual Language Models (VLMs) have shown remarkable advancements in these areas in recent years, enabling domain-specific applications as well as zero-shot open vocabulary tasks, combining multiple domains. However, the required computational complexity poses challenges for their application on edge devices and in the context of Mobile Robotics, especially considering the trade-off between accuracy and inference time. In this paper, we investigate the capabilities of state-of-the-art VLMs for the task of Scene Interpretation and Action Recognition, with special regard to small VLMs capable of being deployed to edge devices in the context of Mobile Robotics. The proposed pipeline is evaluated on a diverse dataset consisting of various real-world cityscape, on-campus and indoor scenarios. The experimental evaluation discusses the potential of these small models on edge devices, with particular emphasis on challenges, weaknesses, inherent model biases and the application of the gained information. Supplementary material is provided via the following repository: https://datahub.rz.rptu.de/hstr-csrl-public/publications/scene-interpretation-on-edge-devices/
tldr: This paper evaluates the performance of small Vision-Language Models on edge devices for scene interpretation and action recognition in mobile robotics, highlighting challenges and potential biases in real-world settings.
tldr_zh: 本文评估了小型视觉语言模型在边缘设备上进行场景解读和动作识别的性能，适用于移动机器人，并强调了真实世界场景中的挑战和潜在偏差。
summary_zh: 视频理解、场景解释和常识推理是极具挑战性的任务，它们能够解释视觉信息，使智能体能够感知环境、与之交互并在其中做出理性的决策。近年来，大型语言模型（LLMs）和视觉语言模型（VLMs）在这些领域取得了显著进展，从而能够实现领域特定的应用以及零样本开放词汇任务，并结合多个领域。然而，所需的计算复杂度对它们在边缘设备上以及在移动机器人环境中的应用提出了挑战，尤其是在考虑准确性和推理时间之间的权衡时。在本文中，我们研究了最先进的VLMs在场景解释和动作识别任务上的能力，特别是考虑了能够在移动机器人环境中部署到边缘设备的小型VLMs。所提出的流程在一个由各种真实世界的城市景观、校园和室内场景组成的多样化数据集上进行了评估。实验评估探讨了这些小型模型在边缘设备上的潜力，特别强调了挑战、弱点、固有的模型偏差以及所获得信息的应用。补充材料通过以下存储库提供：https://datahub.rz.rptu.de/hstr-csrl-public/publications/scene-interpretation-on-edge-devices/

title: LACY: A Vision-Language Model-based Language-Action Cycle for Self-Improving Robotic Manipulation
summary: Learning generalizable policies for robotic manipulation increasingly relies on large-scale models that map language instructions to actions (L2A). However, this one-way paradigm often produces policies that execute tasks without deeper contextual understanding, limiting their ability to generalize or explain their behavior. We argue that the complementary skill of mapping actions back to language (A2L) is essential for developing more holistic grounding. An agent capable of both acting and explaining its actions can form richer internal representations and unlock new paradigms for self-supervised learning. We introduce LACY (Language-Action Cycle), a unified framework that learns such bidirectional mappings within a single vision-language model. LACY is jointly trained on three synergistic tasks: generating parameterized actions from language (L2A), explaining observed actions in language (A2L), and verifying semantic consistency between two language descriptions (L2C). This enables a self-improving cycle that autonomously generates and filters new training data through an active augmentation strategy targeting low-confidence cases, thereby improving the model without additional human labels. Experiments on pick-and-place tasks in both simulation and the real world show that LACY improves task success rates by 56.46% on average and yields more robust language-action grounding for robotic manipulation. Project page: https://vla2026.github.io/LACY/
tldr: The paper introduces LACY, a vision-language model-based framework for robotic manipulation that learns bidirectional language-action mappings (L2A and A2L) and semantic consistency to achieve self-improvement through active data augmentation, demonstrating improved task success in pick-and-place tasks.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了LACY，一个基于视觉-语言模型的机器人操作框架，它学习双向语言-动作映射（L2A和A2L）和语义一致性，通过主动数据增强实现自我改进，并在抓取放置任务中展示了更高的任务成功率。
summary_zh: 用于机器人操作的通用策略学习越来越依赖于将语言指令映射到动作（L2A）的大规模模型。然而，这种单向范式常常产生在缺乏更深层次的上下文理解情况下执行任务的策略，限制了它们泛化或解释其行为的能力。我们认为，将动作映射回语言（A2L）的互补技能对于开发更全面的语义基础至关重要。如果一个智能体能够执行动作并解释其动作，它就可以形成更丰富的内部表征，并为自监督学习解锁新的范式。我们提出了LACY（语言-动作循环），这是一个统一的框架，可以在单个视觉-语言模型中学习这种双向映射。 LACY 在三个协同任务上联合训练：从语言生成参数化动作（L2A）、用语言解释观察到的动作（A2L）以及验证两个语言描述之间的语义一致性（L2C）。这使得一个自我改进的循环能够通过一种针对低置信度案例的主动增强策略自主生成和过滤新的训练数据，从而在无需额外人工标注的情况下改进模型。在模拟和真实世界中抓取放置任务的实验表明，LACY 使任务成功率平均提高了 56.46%，并为机器人操作产生了更鲁棒的语言-动作语义基础。项目主页：https://vla2026.github.io/LACY/

title: Text to Robotic Assembly of Multi Component Objects using 3D Generative AI and Vision Language Models
summary: Advances in 3D generative AI have enabled the creation of physical objects from text prompts, but challenges remain in creating objects involving multiple component types. We present a pipeline that integrates 3D generative AI with vision-language models (VLMs) to enable the robotic assembly of multi-component objects from natural language. Our method leverages VLMs for zero-shot, multi-modal reasoning about geometry and functionality to decompose AI-generated meshes into multi-component 3D models using predefined structural and panel components. We demonstrate that a VLM is capable of determining which mesh regions need panel components in addition to structural components, based on the object's geometry and functionality. Evaluation across test objects shows that users preferred the VLM-generated assignments 90.6% of the time, compared to 59.4% for rule-based and 2.5% for random assignment. Lastly, the system allows users to refine component assignments through conversational feedback, enabling greater human control and agency in making physical objects with generative AI and robotics.
tldr: This paper presents a pipeline using 3D generative AI and vision-language models (VLMs) for robotic assembly of multi-component objects from text, demonstrating VLMs' superior performance in component assignment compared to rule-based and random methods and allowing conversational refinement.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种使用3D生成式AI和视觉-语言模型(VLMs)的管道，用于从文本进行多组件物体的机器人组装，展示了VLM在组件分配方面优于基于规则和随机方法的效果，并允许通过对话进行细化。
summary_zh: 3D生成式人工智能的进步使得通过文本提示创建物理对象成为可能，但涉及多种组件类型的对象创建仍然面临挑战。我们提出了一种管道，将3D生成式人工智能与视觉-语言模型(VLMs)集成，以实现从自然语言进行多组件对象的机器人组装。我们的方法利用VLMs对几何形状和功能进行零样本、多模态推理，从而使用预定义的结构和面板组件将人工智能生成的网格分解为多组件3D模型。我们证明了VLM能够根据对象的几何形状和功能，确定哪些网格区域除了结构组件之外还需要面板组件。对测试对象的评估表明，用户对VLM生成的分配方案的偏好度为90.6%，而基于规则和随机分配的偏好度分别为59.4%和2.5%。最后，该系统允许用户通过对话反馈来优化组件分配，从而在使用生成式人工智能和机器人技术制造物理对象时，实现更大程度的人工控制和自主性。

title: TRACE: Textual Reasoning for Affordance Coordinate Extraction
summary: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) struggle to translate high-level instructions into the precise spatial affordances required for robotic manipulation. While visual Chain-of-Thought (CoT) methods exist, they are often computationally intensive. In this work, we introduce TRACE (Textual Reasoning for Affordance Coordinate Extraction), a novel methodology that integrates a textual Chain of Reasoning (CoR) into the affordance prediction process. We use this methodology to create the TRACE dataset, a large-scale collection created via an autonomous pipeline that pairs instructions with explicit textual rationales. By fine-tuning a VLM on this data, our model learns to externalize its spatial reasoning before acting. Our experiments show that our TRACE-tuned model achieves state-of-the-art performance, reaching 48.1% accuracy on the primary Where2Place (W2P) benchmark (a 9.6% relative improvement) and 55.0% on the more challenging W2P(h) subset. Crucially, an ablation study demonstrates that performance scales directly with the amount of reasoning data used, confirming the CoR's effectiveness. Furthermore, analysis of the model's attention maps reveals an interpretable reasoning process where focus shifts dynamically across reasoning steps. This work shows that training VLMs to generate a textual CoR is an effective and robust strategy for enhancing the precision, reliability, and interpretability of VLM-based robot control. Our dataset and code are available at https://github.com/jink-ucla/TRACE
tldr: The paper introduces TRACE, a novel method that incorporates textual Chain-of-Reasoning (CoR) to improve the affordance prediction of vision-language models (VLMs) for robot manipulation, and presents a dataset created using this method, demonstrating state-of-the-art performance on Where2Place benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了TRACE，一种新颖的方法，通过结合文本推理链（CoR）来提高视觉语言模型（VLMs）在机器人操作中的可供性预测，并提出了使用该方法创建的数据集，在Where2Place基准测试中表现出最先进的性能。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言模型 (VLMs) 难以将高层指令转化为机器人操作所需的精确空间可供性。虽然已存在视觉思维链 (CoT) 方法，但它们通常计算密集。在这项工作中，我们介绍 TRACE (可供性坐标提取的文本推理)，一种将文本推理链 (CoR) 集成到可供性预测过程中的新颖方法。我们使用该方法创建了 TRACE 数据集，这是一个通过自主管道生成的大规模数据集，将指令与显式的文本理由配对。通过在此数据上微调 VLM，我们的模型学习在行动前将空间推理外化。我们的实验表明，经过 TRACE 调优的模型实现了最先进的性能，在主要的 Where2Place (W2P) 基准测试中达到 48.1% 的准确率（相对提升 9.6%），在更具挑战性的 W2P(h) 子集中达到 55.0%。至关重要的是，一项消融研究表明，性能与使用的推理数据量直接相关，证实了 CoR 的有效性。此外，对模型注意力图的分析揭示了一个可解释的推理过程，其中焦点在推理步骤中动态转移。这项工作表明，训练 VLM 生成文本 CoR 是增强基于 VLM 的机器人控制的精度、可靠性和可解释性的有效且稳健的策略。 我们的数据集和代码可在 https://github.com/jink-ucla/TRACE 上获得。

title: The Collaboration Gap
summary: The trajectory of AI development suggests that we will increasingly rely on agent-based systems composed of independently developed agents with different information, privileges, and tools. The success of these systems will critically depend on effective collaboration among these heterogeneous agents, even under partial observability. Despite intense interest, few empirical studies have evaluated such agent-agent collaboration at scale. We propose a collaborative maze-solving benchmark that (i) isolates collaborative capabilities, (ii) modulates problem complexity, (iii) enables scalable automated grading, and (iv) imposes no output-format constraints, preserving ecological plausibility. Using this framework, we evaluate 32 leading open- and closed-source models in solo, homogeneous, and heterogeneous pairings. Our results reveal a "collaboration gap": models that perform well solo often degrade substantially when required to collaborate. Collaboration can break down dramatically; for instance, small distilled models that solve mazes well alone may fail almost completely in certain pairings. We find that starting with the stronger agent often improves outcomes, motivating a "relay inference" approach where the stronger agent leads before handing off to the weaker one, closing much of the gap. Our findings argue for (1) collaboration-aware evaluation, (2) training strategies developed to enhance collaborative capabilities, and (3) interaction design that reliably elicits agents' latent skills, guidance that applies to AI-AI and human-AI collaboration.
tldr: This paper introduces a collaborative maze-solving benchmark to evaluate agent-agent collaboration at scale, revealing a "collaboration gap" where individual agent performance degrades in collaborative settings, and proposes a relay inference approach to mitigate this gap.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一个协作迷宫求解基准，用于大规模评估agent间的协作，揭示了“协作差距”，即个体agent的性能在协作环境中会下降，并提出了一种中继推理方法来弥合这一差距。
summary_zh: 人工智能发展趋势表明，我们将越来越依赖于基于智能体的系统，这些系统由独立开发的、拥有不同信息、权限和工具的智能体组成。这些系统的成功将关键取决于这些异构智能体之间有效的协作，即使在部分可观测性下也是如此。尽管人们对此兴趣浓厚，但很少有实证研究大规模地评估这种智能体-智能体之间的协作。我们提出了一个协作迷宫求解基准，它（i）隔离了协作能力，（ii）调节了问题复杂度，（iii）实现了可扩展的自动评分，以及（iv）没有施加输出格式的约束，从而保留了生态合理性。利用这个框架，我们评估了32个领先的开源和闭源模型在单人、同构和异构配对中的表现。我们的结果揭示了一个“协作差距”：单独表现良好的模型在需要协作时通常会显著下降。协作可能会急剧崩溃；例如，单独求解迷宫效果良好的小型蒸馏模型在某些配对中可能几乎完全失败。我们发现从更强大的智能体开始通常可以改善结果，这激发了一种“接力推理”方法，即更强大的智能体先主导，然后再交给较弱的智能体，从而缩小大部分差距。我们的研究结果论证了(1)协作感知评估，(2)为增强协作能力而开发的训练策略，以及(3)可靠地激发智能体的潜在技能的交互设计，这些指导适用于AI-AI和人-AI协作。

title: Adaptive Neighborhood-Constrained Q Learning for Offline Reinforcement Learning
summary: Offline reinforcement learning (RL) suffers from extrapolation errors induced by out-of-distribution (OOD) actions. To address this, offline RL algorithms typically impose constraints on action selection, which can be systematically categorized into density, support, and sample constraints. However, we show that each category has inherent limitations: density and sample constraints tend to be overly conservative in many scenarios, while the support constraint, though least restrictive, faces challenges in accurately modeling the behavior policy. To overcome these limitations, we propose a new neighborhood constraint that restricts action selection in the Bellman target to the union of neighborhoods of dataset actions. Theoretically, the constraint not only bounds extrapolation errors and distribution shift under certain conditions, but also approximates the support constraint without requiring behavior policy modeling. Moreover, it retains substantial flexibility and enables pointwise conservatism by adapting the neighborhood radius for each data point. In practice, we employ data quality as the adaptation criterion and design an adaptive neighborhood constraint. Building on an efficient bilevel optimization framework, we develop a simple yet effective algorithm, Adaptive Neighborhood-constrained Q learning (ANQ), to perform Q learning with target actions satisfying this constraint. Empirically, ANQ achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard offline RL benchmarks and exhibits strong robustness in scenarios with noisy or limited data.
tldr: This paper introduces Adaptive Neighborhood-constrained Q learning (ANQ) for offline reinforcement learning, which overcomes limitations of existing constraint-based methods by adapting the neighborhood radius for each data point based on data quality, achieving state-of-the-art performance and robustness.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了用于离线强化学习的自适应邻域约束Q学习（ANQ）算法，通过根据数据质量自适应地调整每个数据点的邻域半径，克服了现有基于约束方法局限性，实现了最先进的性能和稳健性。
summary_zh: 离线强化学习(RL)受到分布外(OOD)动作引起的推断错误的影响。为了解决这个问题，离线RL算法通常对动作选择施加约束，这些约束可以系统地分为密度、支持和样本约束。然而，我们表明每个类别都有其固有的局限性：密度和样本约束在许多场景中往往过于保守，而支持约束虽然限制最少，但在准确建模行为策略方面面临挑战。为了克服这些局限性，我们提出了一种新的邻域约束，该约束将贝尔曼目标中的动作选择限制在数据集动作的邻域的并集内。从理论上讲，该约束不仅在特定条件下限制了推断误差和分布偏移，而且无需行为策略建模即可逼近支持约束。此外，它保留了很大的灵活性，并通过调整每个数据点的邻域半径来实现逐点的保守性。在实践中，我们采用数据质量作为自适应标准，并设计了一种自适应邻域约束。基于一个高效的双层优化框架，我们开发了一种简单而有效的算法，自适应邻域约束Q学习（ANQ），以执行满足此约束的目标动作的Q学习。在经验上，ANQ在标准的离线RL基准测试中取得了最先进的性能，并在存在噪声或有限数据的场景中表现出强大的鲁棒性。

title: Extending RLVR to Open-Ended Tasks via Verifiable Multiple-Choice Reformulation
summary: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards(RLVR) has demonstrated great potential in enhancing the reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs). However, its success has thus far been largely confined to the mathematical and programming domains with clear and automatically checkable outcomes. Reinforcement learning on open-ended tasks (e.g., creative writing and subjective Q&A) continues to rely on reward models due to the absence of verifiable solutions. This raises a key question: how can we extend RLVR to strengthen reasoning in open-ended tasks regardless of the absence of the unambiguous ground truth? To overcome this challenge, we introduce Verifiable Multiple-Choice Reformulation for Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (VMR-RLVR), a novel training strategy that restructures open-ended data into verifiable multiple-choice formats, enabling effective training even in the absence of explicit ground truth. Experimental results on multiple benchmarks validate the effectiveness of our method in improving LLM performance on open-ended tasks. Notably, across seven open-ended benchmarks, our VMR-RLVR training delivers an average gain of 3.29 points over the RL with reward model.
tldr: The paper introduces VMR-RLVR, a method to extend Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) to open-ended tasks by reformulating data into a verifiable multiple-choice format, achieving performance gains over reward model based RL on multiple benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了VMR-RLVR，一种通过将数据重构为可验证的多项选择形式，将具有可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）扩展到开放式任务的方法。相较于基于奖励模型的强化学习，该方法在多个基准测试中均取得了性能提升。
summary_zh: 具有可验证奖励的强化学习(RLVR)在增强大型语言模型(LLM)的推理能力方面展现出巨大潜力。然而，其成功至今主要局限于数学和编程领域，这些领域具有明确且可自动检查的结果。由于缺乏可验证的解决方案，开放式任务（例如，创意写作和主观问答）上的强化学习仍然依赖于奖励模型。这就引出了一个关键问题：我们如何在缺乏明确真值的情况下，将RLVR扩展到增强开放式任务中的推理能力？为了克服这一挑战，我们引入了可验证多项选择重构用于基于可验证奖励的强化学习(VMR-RLVR)，这是一种新颖的训练策略，该策略将开放式数据重构为可验证的多项选择形式，从而即使在缺乏显式真值的情况下也能进行有效训练。在多个基准测试上的实验结果验证了我们的方法在提高LLM在开放式任务上的性能方面的有效性。值得注意的是，在七个开放式基准测试中，我们的VMR-RLVR训练比使用奖励模型的强化学习平均提升了3.29个百分点。

title: ReAcTree: Hierarchical LLM Agent Trees with Control Flow for Long-Horizon Task Planning
summary: Recent advancements in large language models (LLMs) have enabled significant progress in decision-making and task planning for embodied autonomous agents. However, most existing methods struggle with complex, long-horizon tasks because they rely on a monolithic trajectory that entangles all past decisions and observations to solve the entire task in a single unified process. To address this limitation, we propose ReAcTree, a hierarchical task-planning method that decomposes a complex goal into manageable subgoals within a dynamically constructed agent tree. Each subgoal is handled by an LLM agent node capable of reasoning, acting, and further expanding the tree, while control flow nodes coordinate the execution strategies of agent nodes. In addition, we integrate two complementary memory systems: each agent node retrieves goal-specific, subgoal-level examples from episodic memory and shares environment-specific observations through working memory. Experiments on the WAH-NL and ALFRED show ReAcTree consistently outperforms strong task-planning baselines such as ReAct across diverse LLMs. Notably, on WAH-NL, ReAcTree achieves a 61% goal success rate with Qwen 2.5 72B, nearly doubling ReAct's 31%. The code is available at https://github.com/Choi-JaeWoo/ReAcTree.git.
tldr: ReAcTree introduces a hierarchical LLM agent tree structure for long-horizon task planning, achieving significant improvements over existing methods like ReAct in complex environments such as WAH-NL and ALFRED.
tldr_zh: ReAcTree 引入了一种用于长周期任务规划的分层 LLM 代理树结构，在 WAH-NL 和 ALFRED 等复杂环境中，相比 ReAct 等现有方法取得了显著改进。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 的最新进展显著推动了具身自主智能体在决策和任务规划方面的进步。然而，现有的大多数方法难以应对复杂、长时程的任务，因为它们依赖于单一的轨迹，将所有过去的决策和观察纠缠在一起，从而在一个统一的过程中解决整个任务。为了解决这一局限性，我们提出了 ReAcTree，一种分层任务规划方法，它将复杂的目标分解为动态构建的智能体树内的可管理子目标。每个子目标由一个 LLM 智能体节点处理，该节点能够进行推理、行动并进一步扩展树，而控制流节点协调智能体节点的执行策略。此外，我们集成了两个互补的记忆系统：每个智能体节点从情景记忆中检索特定于目标的子目标级别示例，并通过工作记忆共享特定于环境的观察。在 WAH-NL 和 ALFRED 上的实验表明，ReAcTree 在各种 LLM 上始终优于强大的任务规划基线，例如 ReAct。值得注意的是，在 WAH-NL 上，ReAcTree 使用 Qwen 2.5 72B 实现了 61% 的目标成功率，几乎是 ReAct 的 31% 的两倍。代码可在 https://github.com/Choi-JaeWoo/ReAcTree.git 获取。

title: V-Agent: An Interactive Video Search System Using Vision-Language Models
summary: We introduce V-Agent, a novel multi-agent platform designed for advanced video search and interactive user-system conversations. By fine-tuning a vision-language model (VLM) with a small video preference dataset and enhancing it with a retrieval vector from an image-text retrieval model, we overcome the limitations of traditional text-based retrieval systems in multimodal scenarios. The VLM-based retrieval model independently embeds video frames and audio transcriptions from an automatic speech recognition (ASR) module into a shared multimodal representation space, enabling V-Agent to interpret both visual and spoken content for context-aware video search. This system consists of three agents-a routing agent, a search agent, and a chat agent-that work collaboratively to address user intents by refining search outputs and communicating with users. The search agent utilizes the VLM-based retrieval model together with an additional re-ranking module to further enhance video retrieval quality. Our proposed framework demonstrates state-of-the-art zero-shot performance on the MultiVENT 2.0 benchmark, highlighting its potential for both academic research and real-world applications. The retrieval model and demo videos are available at https://huggingface.co/NCSOFT/multimodal-embedding.
tldr: V-Agent is a multi-agent video search system that leverages a fine-tuned vision-language model for context-aware video retrieval, achieving state-of-the-art zero-shot performance on the MultiVENT 2.0 benchmark and is available for academic research.
tldr_zh: V-Agent 是一个多代理视频搜索系统，利用微调的视觉-语言模型进行上下文感知的视频检索，在 MultiVENT 2.0 基准测试中实现了最先进的零样本性能，并可用于学术研究。
summary_zh: 我们介绍了V-Agent，一种用于高级视频搜索和交互式用户-系统对话的新型多智能体平台。通过使用小型视频偏好数据集微调视觉-语言模型（VLM），并利用图像-文本检索模型的检索向量增强它，我们克服了传统基于文本的检索系统在多模态场景中的局限性。基于VLM的检索模型独立地将视频帧和来自自动语音识别（ASR）模块的音频转录本嵌入到一个共享的多模态表示空间中，从而使V-Agent能够解释视觉和口语内容，以进行上下文感知的视频搜索。该系统由三个智能体——路由智能体、搜索智能体和聊天智能体——组成，它们协同工作，通过优化搜索结果并与用户沟通来解决用户意图。搜索智能体利用基于VLM的检索模型以及一个额外的重排序模块来进一步提高视频检索质量。我们提出的框架在MultiVENT 2.0基准测试中展示了最先进的零样本性能，突出了其在学术研究和实际应用中的潜力。检索模型和演示视频可在https://huggingface.co/NCSOFT/multimodal-embedding获取。

title: Training Proactive and Personalized LLM Agents
summary: While existing work focuses primarily on task success, we argue that effective real-world agents require optimizing three dimensions: productivity (task completion), proactivity (asking essential questions), and personalization (adapting to diverse user preferences). We introduce UserVille, an interactive environment with LLM-based user simulators enabling diverse, configurable user preferences. Leveraging UserVille, we introduce PPP, a multi-objective reinforcement learning approach that jointly optimizes all three dimensions: Productivity, Proactivity, and Personalization. Experiments on software engineering and deep research tasks show that agents trained with PPP achieve substantial improvements over strong baselines such as GPT-5 (+21.6 on average), demonstrating the ability to ask strategic clarifying questions, adapt to unseen user preferences, and improve task success through better interaction. This work demonstrates that explicitly optimizing for user-centered interaction is critical for building practical and effective AI agents.
tldr: The paper introduces a novel reinforcement learning approach, PPP, trained in a simulated environment (UserVille) to optimize productivity, proactivity, and personalization in LLM agents, demonstrating significant improvements over baselines like GPT-5.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种新的强化学习方法 PPP，它在模拟环境 (UserVille) 中进行训练，以优化 LLM 代理的生产力、主动性和个性化，并展示了相对于 GPT-5 等基线的显着改进。
summary_zh: 现有研究主要关注任务成功率，但我们认为，有效的现实世界智能体需要优化以下三个维度：生产力（任务完成）、主动性（提出关键问题）和个性化（适应不同的用户偏好）。我们引入了UserVille，这是一个交互式环境，包含基于大语言模型的用户模拟器，能够模拟多样化、可配置的用户偏好。基于UserVille，我们提出了PPP，一种多目标强化学习方法，它联合优化所有三个维度：生产力、主动性和个性化。在软件工程和深度研究任务上的实验表明，用PPP训练的智能体比强大的基线模型（如GPT-5，平均提高21.6）取得了显著的改进，展现了提出战略性澄清性问题、适应未见用户偏好以及通过更好的交互提升任务成功率的能力。这项工作证明了显式地优化以用户为中心的交互对于构建实用且有效的AI智能体至关重要。

title: Optimal-Agent-Selection: State-Aware Routing Framework for Efficient Multi-Agent Collaboration
summary: The emergence of multi-agent systems powered by large language models (LLMs) has unlocked new frontiers in complex task-solving, enabling diverse agents to integrate unique expertise, collaborate flexibly, and address challenges unattainable for individual models. However, the full potential of such systems is hindered by rigid agent scheduling and inefficient coordination strategies that fail to adapt to evolving task requirements. In this paper, we propose STRMAC, a state-aware routing framework designed for efficient collaboration in multi-agent systems. Our method separately encodes interaction history and agent knowledge to power the router, which adaptively selects the most suitable single agent at each step for efficient and effective collaboration. Furthermore, we introduce a self-evolving data generation approach that accelerates the collection of high-quality execution paths for efficient system training. Experiments on challenging collaborative reasoning benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance, achieving up to 23.8% improvement over baselines and reducing data collection overhead by up to 90.1% compared to exhaustive search.
tldr: This paper introduces STRMAC, a state-aware routing framework for multi-agent systems using LLMs, which adaptively selects the optimal agent at each step based on interaction history and agent knowledge, achieving state-of-the-art performance and reduced data collection overhead.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了STRMAC，一个基于LLM的多智能体系统中使用的状态感知路由框架。该框架基于交互历史和智能体知识自适应地选择每一步的最佳智能体，实现了最先进的性能并减少了数据收集开销。
summary_zh: 由大型语言模型（LLMs）驱动的多智能体系统的出现，为解决复杂任务开辟了新的前沿，使不同的智能体能够整合独特的专业知识、灵活协作，并应对单个模型无法完成的挑战。然而，这种系统的全部潜力受到僵化的智能体调度和低效的协调策略的阻碍，这些策略无法适应不断演变的任务需求。在本文中，我们提出了STRMAC，一个为多智能体系统中的高效协作而设计的状态感知路由框架。我们的方法分别编码交互历史和智能体知识来驱动路由器，该路由器自适应地选择每一步最合适的单个智能体，以实现高效和有效的协作。此外，我们引入了一种自演化的数据生成方法，能够加速高质量执行路径的收集，以实现高效的系统训练。在具有挑战性的协作推理基准上的实验表明，我们的方法实现了最先进的性能，与基线相比实现了高达23.8%的改进，并且与穷举搜索相比，数据收集开销降低了高达90.1%。

title: Automated Reward Design for Gran Turismo
summary: When designing reinforcement learning (RL) agents, a designer communicates the desired agent behavior through the definition of reward functions - numerical feedback given to the agent as reward or punishment for its actions. However, mapping desired behaviors to reward functions can be a difficult process, especially in complex environments such as autonomous racing. In this paper, we demonstrate how current foundation models can effectively search over a space of reward functions to produce desirable RL agents for the Gran Turismo 7 racing game, given only text-based instructions. Through a combination of LLM-based reward generation, VLM preference-based evaluation, and human feedback we demonstrate how our system can be used to produce racing agents competitive with GT Sophy, a champion-level RL racing agent, as well as generate novel behaviors, paving the way for practical automated reward design in real world applications.
tldr: This paper presents a system leveraging Large Language Models and Vision-Language Models for automated reward design in Gran Turismo 7, creating racing agents competitive with champion-level agents and generating novel behaviors.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种利用大型语言模型和视觉语言模型在Gran Turismo 7中自动设计奖励的系统，该系统可以生成与冠军级智能体竞争的赛车智能体，并产生新颖的行为。
summary_zh: 在设计强化学习（RL）智能体时，设计者通过奖励函数的定义来传达期望的智能体行为 - 给智能体针对其行为的数值反馈，作为奖励或惩罚。然而，将期望的行为映射到奖励函数可能是一个困难的过程，尤其是在像自动驾驶赛车这样复杂的环境中。在本文中，我们展示了当前的基础模型如何在奖励函数空间中进行有效搜索，仅给定基于文本的指令，即可为《Gran Turismo 7》赛车游戏生成理想的强化学习智能体。通过结合基于大型语言模型（LLM）的奖励生成、基于视觉语言模型（VLM）的偏好评估和人工反馈，我们证明了我们的系统可以被用来生成能够与GT Sophy（冠军级强化学习赛车智能体）竞争的赛车智能体，并生成新颖的行为，从而为现实世界应用中实用的自动化奖励设计铺平道路。

title: Natural Building Blocks for Structured World Models: Theory, Evidence, and Scaling
summary: The field of world modeling is fragmented, with researchers developing bespoke architectures that rarely build upon each other. We propose a framework that specifies the natural building blocks for structured world models based on the fundamental stochastic processes that any world model must capture: discrete processes (logic, symbols) and continuous processes (physics, dynamics); the world model is then defined by the hierarchical composition of these building blocks. We examine Hidden Markov Models (HMMs) and switching linear dynamical systems (sLDS) as natural building blocks for discrete and continuous modeling--which become partially-observable Markov decision processes (POMDPs) and controlled sLDS when augmented with actions. This modular approach supports both passive modeling (generation, forecasting) and active control (planning, decision-making) within the same architecture. We avoid the combinatorial explosion of traditional structure learning by largely fixing the causal architecture and searching over only four depth parameters. We review practical expressiveness through multimodal generative modeling (passive) and planning from pixels (active), with performance competitive to neural approaches while maintaining interpretability. The core outstanding challenge is scalable joint structure-parameter learning; current methods finesse this by cleverly growing structure and parameters incrementally, but are limited in their scalability. If solved, these natural building blocks could provide foundational infrastructure for world modeling, analogous to how standardized layers enabled progress in deep learning.
tldr: This paper proposes a modular framework for structured world models using discrete and continuous stochastic processes as building blocks, aiming for scalable joint structure-parameter learning and interpretability, analogous to standardized layers in deep learning.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一个用于构建结构化世界模型的模块化框架，使用离散和连续随机过程作为构建模块，旨在实现可扩展的联合结构-参数学习和可解释性，类似于深度学习中的标准化层。
summary_zh: 世界建模领域目前较为零散，研究人员开发的定制架构很少相互借鉴。我们提出一个框架，该框架基于任何世界模型都必须捕捉的基本随机过程，指定了结构化世界模型的自然构建块：离散过程（逻辑、符号）和连续过程（物理、动力学）；然后，世界模型由这些构建块的层级组合来定义。我们研究了隐马尔可夫模型（HMM）和切换线性动态系统（sLDS）作为离散和连续建模的自然构建块——当增加动作控制时，它们分别变为部分可观测马尔可夫决策过程（POMDP）和受控sLDS。这种模块化方法支持同一架构内的被动建模（生成、预测）和主动控制（规划、决策）。我们通过大致固定因果架构并仅搜索四个深度参数，避免了传统结构学习的组合爆炸。我们通过多模态生成建模（被动）和从像素进行规划（主动）来评估实际的表达能力，其性能与神经方法相比具有竞争力，同时保持了可解释性。目前核心的突出挑战是可扩展的联合结构-参数学习；目前的方法巧妙地通过增量增长结构和参数来解决这个问题，但其可扩展性有限。如果这个问题得到解决，这些自然构建块可以为世界建模提供基础架构，类似于标准化层如何促进深度学习的进步。

title: Human-AI Co-Embodied Intelligence for Scientific Experimentation and Manufacturing
summary: Scientific experimentation and manufacturing rely on prolonged protocol development and complex, multi-step implementation, which require continuous human expertise for precise execution and decision-making, limiting interpretability and scalability. Here, we introduce human-artificial intelligence (AI) co-embodied intelligence, a new form of physical AI that unites human researchers, agentic AI, and wearable hardware. In this paradigm, humans provide precise execution, while agentic AI contributes contextual reasoning, adaptive planning, and analysis. The wearable interface continuously captures experimentation and manufacturing, facilitating seamless communication between humans and AI. We instantiate this paradigm in a microfabrication cleanroom, leading to the agentic-physical experimentation (APEX) system which understands fabrication procedure with accuracy 51% higher than state-of-the-art multimodal large language models/vision language models (LLMs/VLMs), detects and corrects fabrication errors in real-time, and transfers procedural expertise to novice users. Critically, APEX system enables the co-development of fabrication protocols in cleanrooms, overcoming the incompatibility of elastomeric materials in standard microfabrication processes and enabling previously unattainable fabrication outcomes, as demonstrated by the wafer-scale realization of brain-level soft neural probe capable of single-unit-resolution neural recording. These results establish the human-AI co-embodied intelligence that extends agentic reasoning beyond computation into the physical domain, transforming scientific experimentation and manufacturing into autonomous, traceable, interpretable and scalable processes.
tldr: This paper introduces human-AI co-embodied intelligence, specifically APEX, a system combining human expertise with AI reasoning and wearable interfaces for scientific experimentation and manufacturing, achieving improved accuracy and novel fabrication outcomes in a microfabrication cleanroom.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种人机共生智能，即 APEX 系统，它结合了人类的专业知识、人工智能推理和可穿戴界面，用于科学实验和制造。该系统在微制造洁净室中实现了更高的精度和新颖的制造结果。
summary_zh: 科学实验和制造依赖于漫长的协议开发和复杂的多步骤实施，这需要持续的人工专业知识来进行精确执行和决策，从而限制了解释性和可扩展性。在此，我们介绍了人-人工智能（AI）共生智能，一种新型的物理AI，它将人类研究者、自主型AI和可穿戴硬件结合起来。在这种模式中，人类提供精确的执行，而自主型AI则贡献上下文推理、自适应规划和分析。可穿戴界面持续捕获实验和制造过程，促进人类和AI之间的无缝通信。我们在微制造洁净室中实例化了这种模式，产生了自主型-物理实验（APEX）系统，该系统对制造程序的理解准确率比最先进的多模态大型语言模型/视觉语言模型（LLMs/VLMs）高出51%，能够实时检测和纠正制造错误，并将程序专业知识转移给新手用户。至关重要的是，APEX系统能够在洁净室中协同开发制造协议，克服了标准微制造工艺中弹性体材料的不相容性，并实现了以前无法达到的制造结果，如通过晶圆级实现能够进行单单元分辨率神经记录的脑级软神经探针所证明的那样。这些结果确立了人-AI共生智能，它将自主型推理从计算扩展到物理领域，将科学实验和制造转化为自主、可追溯、可解释和可扩展的过程。

title: Simulating Environments with Reasoning Models for Agent Training
summary: LLM agents excel in compact environments requiring deep reasoning but remain brittle when operating in broader, more complex contexts that demand robustness across diverse tools and schemas. Building bespoke environments for training is heavy, brittle, and limits progress. In this paper, we demonstrate that LLMs can simulate realistic environment feedback without access to actual testbed data or APIs. Inspired by this capability, we propose two frameworks: Simia-SFT, a pipeline that synthesizes SFT data by amplifying small seed sets into diverse trajectories in an environment-agnostic manner, and Simia-RL, a framework that enables RL training without real environment implementations through LLM-simulated feedback. Fine-tuning open models yields consistent improvements across multiple benchmarks, surpassing GPT-4o and approaching o4-mini on $τ^2$-Bench. Together, Simia-SFT and Simia-RL enable scalable agent training without environment engineering, replacing heavy and brittle implementations with flexible LLM-based simulation.
tldr: This paper introduces Simia-SFT and Simia-RL, frameworks using LLMs to simulate environment feedback for agent training, achieving performance improvements and reducing reliance on real-world environments.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了Simia-SFT和Simia-RL，这两个框架使用LLM来模拟环境反馈以进行智能体训练，从而提高了性能并减少了对真实环境的依赖。
summary_zh: LLM智能体在需要深度推理的紧凑环境中表现出色，但在更广泛、更复杂的环境中运行时仍然脆弱，这些环境需要跨越不同工具和模式的鲁棒性。构建用于训练的定制环境既繁重又脆弱，并限制了进展。在本文中，我们证明了LLM可以在无需访问实际测试台数据或API的情况下模拟真实的环境反馈。受此能力的启发，我们提出了两个框架：Simia-SFT，一个通过以环境无关的方式将小型种子集合放大为多样化轨迹来合成SFT数据的流水线；以及Simia-RL，一个通过LLM模拟的反馈来实现无需真实环境实现的RL训练的框架。对开放模型进行微调可以在多个基准测试中产生一致的改进，超越GPT-4o，并在$τ^2$-Bench上接近o4-mini。总而言之，Simia-SFT和Simia-RL实现了无需环境工程的可扩展智能体训练，用灵活的基于LLM的模拟取代了繁重而脆弱的实现。

title: Multimodal Reasoning via Latent Refocusing
summary: Chain of Thought (CoT) reasoning enhances logical performance by decomposing complex tasks, yet its multimodal extension faces a trade-off. The existing Thinking with Images paradigm is limited by the modality gap between vision and language, which hinders reliable extraction of reasoning relevant information from high dimensional visual data. Recent latent space reasoning method provides stronger multimodal representations, but it often lacks the ability to refocus on visual inputs and suffers from limited interpretability. To address these issues, we propose \underline{La}tent \underline{Re}focusing (LaRe), a novel multimodal reasoning paradigm that combines visual refocusing with rich latent representations, enabling iterative reasoning within the latent space. We further design a semantic augmentation training strategy that enhances the semantic structure of the latent space through joint alignment and reconstruction objectives. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that LaRe improves average accuracy by 9.4\% compared to existing baselines while reducing the number of tokens required for inference by 16.5\%. When scaled to a 7B-parameter Large Language Model backbone, LaRe achieves performance comparable to state-of-the-art models and outperforms larger-scale models on almost all benchmarks. Code and checkpoints will be released later.
tldr: The paper introduces LaRe, a multimodal reasoning paradigm that enhances latent space reasoning by integrating visual refocusing and semantic augmentation training, achieving improved accuracy and efficiency compared to existing methods on vision-language tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为LaRe的多模态推理范式，通过整合视觉重聚焦和语义增强训练，增强了潜在空间推理能力，在视觉语言任务上实现了比现有方法更高的准确性和效率。
summary_zh: 思维链（CoT）推理通过分解复杂任务来增强逻辑性能，但其多模态扩展面临着权衡。现有的图像思维范式受限于视觉和语言之间的模态差距，这阻碍了从高维视觉数据中可靠地提取与推理相关的信息。最近的潜在空间推理方法提供了更强的多模态表示，但它通常缺乏重新聚焦于视觉输入的能力，并且可解释性有限。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一种新型的多模态推理范式——潜在重聚焦（LaRe），它结合了视觉重聚焦和丰富的潜在表示，从而能够在潜在空间内进行迭代推理。我们进一步设计了一种语义增强训练策略，通过联合对齐和重构目标来增强潜在空间的语义结构。实验评估表明，与现有的基线相比，LaRe 提高了平均准确率 9.4%，同时减少了推理所需的 tokens 数量 16.5%。当扩展到具有 70 亿参数的大型语言模型主干时，LaRe 的性能可与最先进的模型相媲美，并在几乎所有基准测试中优于更大规模的模型。代码和检查点将在稍后发布。

title: SAIL-RL: Guiding MLLMs in When and How to Think via Dual-Reward RL Tuning
summary: We introduce SAIL-RL, a reinforcement learning (RL) post-training framework that enhances the reasoning capabilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) by teaching them when and how to think. Existing approaches are limited by outcome-only supervision, which rewards correct answers without ensuring sound reasoning, and by uniform thinking strategies, which often lead to overthinking on simple tasks and underthinking on complex ones. SAIL-RL addresses these challenges with a dual reward system: the Thinking Reward, which evaluates reasoning quality through factual grounding, logical coherence, and answer consistency, and the Judging Reward, which adaptively determines whether deep reasoning or direct answering is appropriate. Experiments on the state-of-the-art SAIL-VL2 show that SAIL-RL improves reasoning and multimodal understanding benchmarks at both 4B and 8B scales, achieving competitive performance against commercial closed-source models such as GPT-4o, and substantially reduces hallucinations, establishing it as a principled framework for building more reliable and adaptive MLLMs. The code will be available at https://github.com/BytedanceDouyinContent/SAIL-RL.
tldr: SAIL-RL introduces a reinforcement learning framework that improves MLLM reasoning by using a dual-reward system (Thinking Reward and Judging Reward) to teach models when and how to think, improving performance and reducing hallucinations.
tldr_zh: SAIL-RL 引入了一个强化学习框架，通过使用双重奖励系统（思考奖励和判断奖励）来教导 MLLM 何时以及如何思考，从而提高 MLLM 的推理能力，提高性能并减少幻觉。
summary_zh: 我们提出了SAIL-RL，一种强化学习（RL）后训练框架，通过教导多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）何时以及如何思考，从而增强其推理能力。现有方法受限于仅基于结果的监督，该监督只奖励正确答案而不确保合理的推理，以及受限于统一的思考策略，这通常会导致在简单任务上过度思考，而在复杂任务上思考不足。SAIL-RL通过双重奖励系统解决这些挑战：思考奖励，通过事实依据、逻辑连贯性和答案一致性来评估推理质量；以及判断奖励，自适应地确定深度推理或直接回答是否合适。在最先进的SAIL-VL2上的实验表明，SAIL-RL在4B和8B规模上均提高了推理和多模态理解基准测试的性能，实现了与GPT-4o等商业闭源模型具有竞争力的性能，并大幅减少了幻觉，从而将其确立为构建更可靠和自适应的MLLMs的原则性框架。代码将在https://github.com/BytedanceDouyinContent/SAIL-RL上提供。

title: Pinpointing Trigger Moment for Grounded Video QA: Enhancing Spatio-temporal Grounding in Multimodal Large Language Models
summary: In this technical report, we introduce a framework to address Grounded Video Question Answering (GVQA) task for the ICCV 2025 Perception Test Challenge. The GVQA task demands robust multimodal models capable of complex reasoning over video content, grounding the resulting answers visually, and tracking the referenced objects temporally. To achieve this capability, our proposed approach decomposes the GVQA task into a three-stage pipeline: (1) Video Reasoning \& QA, (2) Spatio-temporal Grounding and (3) Tracking. Our key contribution is the introduction of a trigger moment, derived from our proposed CORTEX prompt, which pinpoints the single most visible frame of a target object to serve as a robust anchor for grounding and tracking. To this end, we achieve the HOTA score of 0.4968, which marks a significant improvement over the previous year's winning score of 0.2704 on GVQA task.
tldr: This paper introduces a three-stage pipeline for Grounded Video Question Answering (GVQA), using a 'trigger moment' derived from a new prompt to improve spatio-temporal grounding and tracking, achieving a significantly higher HOTA score than the previous winning entry.
tldr_zh: 这篇论文介绍了一个用于基于视频的问题回答(GVQA)的三阶段流程，使用从新提示中导出的“触发时刻”来改进时空定位和跟踪，实现了比上届冠军高得多的HOTA分数。
summary_zh: 在本技术报告中，我们介绍了一个框架，旨在解决 ICCV 2025 感知测试挑战赛中的实体视频问答（GVQA）任务。GVQA 任务要求稳健的多模态模型能够对视频内容进行复杂的推理，对生成的答案进行视觉定位，并对引用的对象进行时间上的跟踪。为了实现这一能力，我们提出的方法将 GVQA 任务分解为三个阶段的流程：（1）视频推理与问答，（2）时空定位，以及（3）跟踪。我们主要的贡献在于引入了一个触发时刻，该触发时刻源于我们提出的 CORTEX 提示，它可以精确定位目标对象最明显的单帧，作为定位和跟踪的强大锚点。为此，我们实现了 0.4968 的 HOTA 分数，这标志着比去年 GVQA 任务冠军的 0.2704 分数有了显著的提升。

title: TIR-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Agentic Thinking-with-Images Reasoning
summary: The frontier of visual reasoning is shifting toward models like OpenAI o3, which can intelligently create and operate tools to transform images for problem-solving, also known as thinking-\textit{with}-images in chain-of-thought. Yet existing benchmarks fail to fully capture this advanced capability. Even Visual Search, the most common benchmark for current thinking-\textit{with}-images methods, tests only basic operations such as localization and cropping, offering little insight into more complex, dynamic, and tool-dependent reasoning. We introduce \textbf{TIR-Bench}, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating agentic thinking-with-images across 13 diverse tasks, each requiring novel tool use for image processing and manipulation in chain-of-thought. We evaluate 22 multimodal large language models (MLLMs), from leading open-sourced and proprietary models to those with explicit tool-use augmentation. Results show that TIR-Bench is universally challenging, and strong performance requires genuine thinking-with-images capabilities. Finally, we present a pilot study comparing direct versus agentic fine-tuning.
tldr: The paper introduces TIR-Bench, a new benchmark designed to evaluate the thinking-with-images reasoning capabilities of multimodal large language models, revealing the limitations of existing benchmarks and the challenges faced by current models.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了TIR-Bench，一个新的基准测试，旨在评估多模态大型语言模型的“图像思考”推理能力，揭示了现有基准测试的局限性以及当前模型面临的挑战。
summary_zh: 视觉推理的前沿正转向 OpenAI o3 等模型，它们能够智能地创建和操作工具来转换图像以解决问题，也称为链式思考中的“与图像一起思考”。然而，现有的基准未能充分捕捉到这种先进的能力。即使视觉搜索（Visual Search），作为当前“与图像一起思考”方法最常见的基准，也只测试定位和裁剪等基本操作，对于更复杂、动态和工具依赖的推理提供的洞察力很有限。我们推出了 \textbf{TIR-Bench}，一个全面的基准，用于评估跨 13 项不同任务的代理式与图像一起思考能力，每项任务都需要以链式思考的方式使用新的工具进行图像处理和操作。我们评估了 22 个多模态大型语言模型 (MLLM)，包括领先的开源和专有模型，以及那些经过明确工具使用增强的模型。结果表明，TIR-Bench 具有普遍的挑战性，而强大的性能需要真正的与图像一起思考能力。最后，我们展示了一个试点研究，比较直接微调和代理式微调。

title: From Solo to Symphony: Orchestrating Multi-Agent Collaboration with Single-Agent Demos
summary: Training a team of agents from scratch in multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) is highly inefficient, much like asking beginners to play a symphony together without first practicing solo. Existing methods, such as offline or transferable MARL, can ease this burden, but they still rely on costly multi-agent data, which often becomes the bottleneck. In contrast, solo experiences are far easier to obtain in many important scenarios, e.g., collaborative coding, household cooperation, and search-and-rescue. To unlock their potential, we propose Solo-to-Collaborative RL (SoCo), a framework that transfers solo knowledge into cooperative learning. SoCo first pretrains a shared solo policy from solo demonstrations, then adapts it for cooperation during multi-agent training through a policy fusion mechanism that combines an MoE-like gating selector and an action editor. Experiments across diverse cooperative tasks show that SoCo significantly boosts the training efficiency and performance of backbone algorithms. These results demonstrate that solo demonstrations provide a scalable and effective complement to multi-agent data, making cooperative learning more practical and broadly applicable.
tldr: The paper introduces Solo-to-Collaborative RL (SoCo), a framework that leverages solo demonstrations to improve the efficiency and performance of multi-agent reinforcement learning by pretraining a shared solo policy and then adapting it for cooperation through policy fusion.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为Solo-to-Collaborative RL (SoCo) 的框架，该框架利用单人演示来提高多智能体强化学习的效率和性能，通过预训练一个共享的单人策略，然后通过策略融合将其调整为合作。
summary_zh: 在多智能体强化学习（MARL）中，从零开始训练一个智能体团队效率极低，就像要求初学者在没有单独练习的情况下一起演奏交响乐一样。现有的方法，如离线或可迁移的MARL，可以减轻这种负担，但它们仍然依赖于代价高昂的多智能体数据，这通常会成为瓶颈。相比之下，在许多重要场景中，如协同编码、家庭合作和搜救，单独经验的获取要容易得多。为了释放它们的潜力，我们提出了Solo-to-Collaborative RL (SoCo)，一个将单独知识迁移到合作学习中的框架。SoCo首先从单独演示中预训练一个共享的单独策略，然后通过一个策略融合机制将其调整为在多智能体训练期间进行合作，该机制结合了一个类似MoE的门控选择器和一个动作编辑器。在各种合作任务中的实验表明，SoCo显著提高了骨干算法的训练效率和性能。这些结果表明，单独演示为多智能体数据提供了一种可扩展且有效的补充，使合作学习更实用，适用范围更广。

title: Agentic World Modeling for 6G: Near-Real-Time Generative State-Space Reasoning
summary: We argue that sixth-generation (6G) intelligence is not fluent token prediction but the capacity to imagine and choose -- to simulate future scenarios, weigh trade-offs, and act with calibrated uncertainty. We reframe open radio access network (O-RAN) near-real-time (Near-RT) control via counterfactual dynamics and a world modeling (WM) paradigm that learns an action-conditioned generative state space. This enables quantitative "what-if" forecasting beyond large language models (LLMs) as the primary modeling primitive. Actions such as physical resource blocks (PRBs) are treated as first-class control inputs in a causal world model, and both aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty are modeled for prediction and what-if analysis. An agentic, model predictive control (MPC)-based cross-entropy method (CEM) planner operates over short horizons, using prior-mean rollouts within data-driven PRB bounds to maximize a deterministic reward. The model couples multi-scale structured state-space mixtures (MS3M) with a compact stochastic latent to form WM-MS3M, summarizing key performance indicators (KPIs) histories and predicting next-step KPIs under hypothetical PRB sequences. On realistic O-RAN traces, WM-MS3M cuts mean absolute error (MAE) by 1.69% versus MS3M with 32% fewer parameters and similar latency, and achieves 35-80% lower root mean squared error (RMSE) than attention/hybrid baselines with 2.3-4.1x faster inference, enabling rare-event simulation and offline policy screening.
tldr: This paper proposes a world modeling approach (WM-MS3M) for near-real-time control in 6G O-RAN, using agentic MPC to simulate future scenarios and optimize resource allocation, demonstrating improved performance over existing methods.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种用于6G O-RAN中近实时控制的世界建模方法 (WM-MS3M)，使用智能MPC来模拟未来场景并优化资源分配，结果表明该方法优于现有方法。
summary_zh: 我们认为，第六代（6G）智能并非流畅的令牌预测，而是想象和选择的能力——模拟未来场景、权衡取舍，以及在校准的不确定性下行动。我们通过反事实动态和世界模型（WM）范式，将开放无线接入网络（O-RAN）近实时（Near-RT）控制重新定义为学习一个以动作为条件的生成状态空间。这使得超越大型语言模型（LLM），作为主要建模单元的定量“如果……会怎样”的预测成为可能。诸如物理资源块（PRB）之类的动作被视为因果世界模型中的一等控制输入，并且对偶然不确定性和认知不确定性都进行了建模，以用于预测和“如果……会怎样”的分析。一种基于代理的、基于模型预测控制（MPC）的交叉熵方法（CEM）规划器在短时域内运行，在数据驱动的PRB约束内使用先验均值展开来最大化确定性奖励。该模型将多尺度结构化状态空间混合（MS3M）与紧凑的随机潜在变量结合起来，形成WM-MS3M，从而总结关键绩效指标（KPI）历史记录，并预测假设PRB序列下的下一步KPI。在真实的O-RAN跟踪数据上，WM-MS3M相对于MS3M，在参数减少32%且延迟相似的情况下，将平均绝对误差（MAE）降低了1.69%，并且比注意力/混合基线实现了低35-80%的均方根误差（RMSE），同时实现了2.3-4.1倍的更快推理速度，从而能够进行罕见事件模拟和离线策略筛选。

title: Curriculum Design for Trajectory-Constrained Agent: Compressing Chain-of-Thought Tokens in LLMs
summary: Training agents to operate under strict constraints during deployment, such as limited resource budgets or stringent safety requirements, presents significant challenges, especially when these constraints render the task complex. In this work, we propose a curriculum learning strategy that gradually tightens constraints during training, enabling the agent to incrementally master the deployment requirements. Inspired by self-paced learning techniques in unconstrained reinforcement learning (RL), our approach facilitates a smoother transition to challenging environments by initially training on simplified versions of the constraints and progressively introducing the full deployment conditions. We provide a theoretical analysis using an RL agent in a binary-tree Markov Decision Process (MDP) to demonstrate that our curriculum strategy can accelerate training relative to a baseline approach that imposes the trajectory constraints from the outset. Moreover, we empirically validate the effectiveness and generality of our method across both RL and large language model (LLM) agents in diverse settings, including a binary-tree MDP, a multi-task navigation domain, and a math reasoning task with two benchmarks. These results highlight the potential of curriculum design in enhancing the efficiency and performance of agents operating under complex trajectory constraints during deployment. Moreover, when applied to LLMs, our strategy enables compression of output chain-of-thought tokens, achieving a substantial inference speedup on consumer hardware, demonstrating its effectiveness for resource-constrained deployment.
tldr: This paper introduces a curriculum learning strategy that gradually tightens trajectory constraints during training, improving the efficiency of RL and LLM agents in constrained environments. The approach also enables compression of chain-of-thought tokens in LLMs, leading to faster inference.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种课程学习策略，通过在训练过程中逐步加强轨迹约束，从而提高RL和LLM智能体在约束环境下的效率。该方法还能够压缩LLM中的思维链令牌，从而实现更快的推理。
summary_zh: 在部署期间训练智能体在严格约束下运行（例如有限的资源预算或严格的安全要求）面临重大挑战，尤其是在这些约束使得任务复杂时。 本文提出一种课程学习策略，在训练期间逐步收紧约束，使智能体能够逐步掌握部署要求。受到无约束强化学习（RL）中自步学习技术的启发，我们的方法通过首先在简化的约束版本上进行训练，然后逐步引入完整的部署条件，从而促进向具有挑战性的环境的平稳过渡。我们使用二叉树马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）中的 RL 智能体进行了理论分析，表明我们的课程策略可以加速训练，相对于从一开始就施加轨迹约束的基线方法。此外，我们在各种设置中对 RL 和大型语言模型（LLM）智能体的有效性和通用性进行了实证验证，这些设置包括二叉树 MDP、多任务导航环境和具有两个基准的数学推理任务。这些结果突显了课程设计在提高智能体在部署期间在复杂轨迹约束下运行的效率和性能方面的潜力。此外，当应用于 LLM 时，我们的策略能够压缩输出的思维链 token，在消费级硬件上实现显着的推理速度提升，证明了其在资源受限部署中的有效性。

title: LUMA-RAG: Lifelong Multimodal Agents with Provably Stable Streaming Alignment
summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has emerged as the dominant paradigm for grounding large language model outputs in verifiable evidence. However, as modern AI agents transition from static knowledge bases to continuous multimodal streams encompassing text, images, video, and audio, two critical challenges arise: maintaining index freshness without prohibitive re-indexing costs, and preserving cross-modal semantic consistency across heterogeneous embedding spaces. We present LUMA-RAG, a lifelong multimodal agent architecture featuring three key innovations: (i) a streaming, multi-tier memory system that dynamically spills embeddings from a hot HNSW tier to a compressed IVFPQ tier under strict memory budgets; (ii) a streaming CLAP->CLIP alignment bridge that maintains cross-modal consistency through incremental orthogonal Procrustes updates; and (iii) stability-aware retrieval telemetry providing Safe@k guarantees by jointly bounding alignment drift and quantization error. Experiments demonstrate robust text-to-image retrieval (Recall@10 = 0.94), graceful performance degradation under product quantization offloading, and provably stable audio-to-image rankings (Safe@1 = 1.0), establishing LUMA-RAG as a practical framework for production multimodal RAG systems.
tldr: LUMA-RAG introduces a novel architecture for lifelong multimodal RAG agents, addressing index freshness and cross-modal consistency in streaming data through a multi-tier memory system, CLAP->CLIP alignment, and stability-aware retrieval.
tldr_zh: LUMA-RAG 提出了一种新的终身多模态 RAG 代理架构，通过多层内存系统、CLAP->CLIP 对齐和感知稳定性的检索，解决了流式数据中的索引新鲜度和跨模态一致性问题。
summary_zh: 检索增强生成 (RAG) 已成为利用可验证证据来确定大型语言模型输出的支配性范式。然而，随着现代人工智能代理从静态知识库过渡到包含文本、图像、视频和音频的连续多模态流，出现了两个关键挑战：在不产生过高重新索引成本的情况下保持索引的新鲜度，以及在异构嵌入空间中保持跨模态的语义一致性。 我们提出了 LUMA-RAG，一种终身多模态代理架构，具有三个关键创新点：（i）一种流式、多层存储系统，可在严格的内存预算下，将嵌入从热 HNSW 层动态溢出到压缩的 IVFPQ 层；（ii）一个流式 CLAP->CLIP 对齐桥，通过增量正交普氏分析更新来保持跨模态一致性；(iii) 稳定性感知检索遥测技术，通过联合限制对齐漂移和量化误差来提供 Safe@k 保证。实验表明，文本到图像检索具有鲁棒性（Recall@10 = 0.94），在乘积量化卸载下具有优雅的性能降级，并且音频到图像排序具有可证明的稳定性（Safe@1 = 1.0），这确立了 LUMA-RAG 作为生产多模态 RAG 系统的实用框架。

title: Learning Interactive World Model for Object-Centric Reinforcement Learning
summary: Agents that understand objects and their interactions can learn policies that are more robust and transferable. However, most object-centric RL methods factor state by individual objects while leaving interactions implicit. We introduce the Factored Interactive Object-Centric World Model (FIOC-WM), a unified framework that learns structured representations of both objects and their interactions within a world model. FIOC-WM captures environment dynamics with disentangled and modular representations of object interactions, improving sample efficiency and generalization for policy learning. Concretely, FIOC-WM first learns object-centric latents and an interaction structure directly from pixels, leveraging pre-trained vision encoders. The learned world model then decomposes tasks into composable interaction primitives, and a hierarchical policy is trained on top: a high level selects the type and order of interactions, while a low level executes them. On simulated robotic and embodied-AI benchmarks, FIOC-WM improves policy-learning sample efficiency and generalization over world-model baselines, indicating that explicit, modular interaction learning is crucial for robust control.
tldr: The paper introduces a Factored Interactive Object-Centric World Model (FIOC-WM) that learns structured representations of objects and their interactions, improving sample efficiency and generalization in reinforcement learning tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种分解交互式以对象为中心的世界模型 (FIOC-WM)，该模型学习对象及其交互的结构化表示，从而提高强化学习任务中的样本效率和泛化能力。
summary_zh: 能够理解物体及其交互方式的智能体可以学习到更具鲁棒性和可迁移性的策略。然而，大多数以物体为中心的强化学习方法通过个体物体来分解状态，而忽略了隐式的交互信息。我们提出了因子化交互式物体中心世界模型（FIOC-WM），这是一个统一的框架，用于学习世界模型中物体及其交互的结构化表示。FIOC-WM利用解耦和模块化的物体交互表示来捕获环境动态，从而提高策略学习的样本效率和泛化能力。具体来说，FIOC-WM首先利用预训练的视觉编码器，直接从像素中学习以物体为中心的隐变量和交互结构。然后，学习到的世界模型将任务分解为可组合的交互原语，并在其基础上训练一个分层策略：高层选择交互的类型和顺序，而低层执行这些交互。在模拟机器人和具身人工智能的基准测试中，FIOC-WM在策略学习的样本效率和泛化能力方面优于世界模型基线，表明显式、模块化的交互学习对于鲁棒控制至关重要。

title: Keeping it Local, Tiny and Real: Automated Report Generation on Edge Computing Devices for Mechatronic-Based Cognitive Systems
summary: Recent advancements in Deep Learning enable hardware-based cognitive systems, that is, mechatronic systems in general and robotics in particular with integrated Artificial Intelligence, to interact with dynamic and unstructured environments. While the results are impressive, the application of such systems to critical tasks like autonomous driving as well as service and care robotics necessitate the evaluation of large amount of heterogeneous data. Automated report generation for Mobile Robotics can play a crucial role in facilitating the evaluation and acceptance of such systems in various domains. In this paper, we propose a pipeline for generating automated reports in natural language utilizing various multi-modal sensors that solely relies on local models capable of being deployed on edge computing devices, thus preserving the privacy of all actors involved and eliminating the need for external services. In particular, we evaluate our implementation on a diverse dataset spanning multiple domains including indoor, outdoor and urban environments, providing quantitative as well as qualitative evaluation results. Various generated example reports and other supplementary materials are available via a public repository.
tldr: This paper presents a pipeline for automated report generation in natural language for robotics on edge devices, focusing on preserving privacy and eliminating reliance on external services. They evaluate the pipeline on a diverse, multi-domain dataset.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种在边缘设备上为机器人生成自然语言自动报告的流程，重点在于保护隐私并消除对外部服务的依赖。他们在多样化的多域数据集上评估了该流程。
summary_zh: 深度学习的最新进展使得基于硬件的认知系统，即通常意义上的机电系统，特别是集成人工智能的机器人，能够与动态和非结构化环境交互。尽管成果令人印象深刻，但此类系统在诸如自动驾驶以及服务型和护理型机器人等关键任务中的应用，需要评估大量异构数据。用于移动机器人的自动报告生成，可以在促进此类系统在各个领域的评估和接受方面发挥关键作用。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于生成自然语言自动报告的管道，该管道利用各种多模态传感器，并且完全依赖于能够部署在边缘计算设备上的本地模型，从而保护所有相关参与者的隐私，并消除对外部服务的需求。 特别地，我们在跨越多个领域的不同数据集上评估我们的实现，包括室内、室外和城市环境，并提供定量以及定性的评估结果。各种生成的示例报告和其他补充材料可通过公共存储库获得。

title: Dexterous Robotic Piano Playing at Scale
summary: Endowing robot hands with human-level dexterity has been a long-standing goal in robotics. Bimanual robotic piano playing represents a particularly challenging task: it is high-dimensional, contact-rich, and requires fast, precise control. We present OmniPianist, the first agent capable of performing nearly one thousand music pieces via scalable, human-demonstration-free learning. Our approach is built on three core components. First, we introduce an automatic fingering strategy based on Optimal Transport (OT), allowing the agent to autonomously discover efficient piano-playing strategies from scratch without demonstrations. Second, we conduct large-scale Reinforcement Learning (RL) by training more than 2,000 agents, each specialized in distinct music pieces, and aggregate their experience into a dataset named RP1M++, consisting of over one million trajectories for robotic piano playing. Finally, we employ a Flow Matching Transformer to leverage RP1M++ through large-scale imitation learning, resulting in the OmniPianist agent capable of performing a wide range of musical pieces. Extensive experiments and ablation studies highlight the effectiveness and scalability of our approach, advancing dexterous robotic piano playing at scale.
tldr: This paper introduces OmniPianist, a robot that can play nearly one thousand piano pieces using reinforcement learning, optimal transport for fingering, and a Flow Matching Transformer for imitation learning from a million-trajectory dataset.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了OmniPianist，一种可以演奏近一千首钢琴曲的机器人，它利用强化学习，基于最优传输的指法策略，以及用于从百万轨迹数据集中进行模仿学习的Flow Matching Transformer。
summary_zh: 赋予机器人手人类级别的灵巧性一直是机器人学领域的一个长期目标。双臂机器人钢琴演奏代表了一项极具挑战性的任务：它是高维度的、富含接触的，并且需要快速、精确的控制。我们提出了OmniPianist，这是第一个能够通过可扩展、无需人类演示的学习方法演奏近1000首乐曲的智能体。我们的方法建立在三个核心组件之上。首先，我们引入了一种基于最优传输（OT）的自动指法策略，使智能体能够从头开始自主发现高效的钢琴演奏策略，而无需任何演示。其次，我们通过训练超过2000个智能体来进行大规模强化学习（RL），每个智能体都专注于不同的乐曲，并将它们的经验汇总成一个名为RP1M++的数据集，其中包含超过一百万条机器人钢琴演奏轨迹。最后，我们采用流匹配Transformer来利用RP1M++进行大规模模仿学习，从而产生能够演奏各种乐曲的OmniPianist智能体。大量的实验和消融研究突出了我们方法的有效性和可扩展性，从而大规模地推进了灵巧的机器人钢琴演奏。

title: Whole-body motion planning and safety-critical control for aerial manipulation
summary: Aerial manipulation combines the maneuverability of multirotors with the dexterity of robotic arms to perform complex tasks in cluttered spaces. Yet planning safe, dynamically feasible trajectories remains difficult due to whole-body collision avoidance and the conservativeness of common geometric abstractions such as bounding boxes or ellipsoids. We present a whole-body motion planning and safety-critical control framework for aerial manipulators built on superquadrics (SQs). Using an SQ-plus-proxy representation, we model both the vehicle and obstacles with differentiable, geometry-accurate surfaces. Leveraging this representation, we introduce a maximum-clearance planner that fuses Voronoi diagrams with an equilibrium-manifold formulation to generate smooth, collision-aware trajectories. We further design a safety-critical controller that jointly enforces thrust limits and collision avoidance via high-order control barrier functions. In simulation, our approach outperforms sampling-based planners in cluttered environments, producing faster, safer, and smoother trajectories and exceeding ellipsoid-based baselines in geometric fidelity. Actual experiments on a physical aerial-manipulation platform confirm feasibility and robustness, demonstrating consistent performance across simulation and hardware settings. The video can be found at https://youtu.be/hQYKwrWf1Ak.
tldr: This paper presents a whole-body motion planning and safety-critical control framework for aerial manipulators using superquadrics for collision avoidance, demonstrating improved performance in simulation and hardware experiments.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种基于超二次曲面的空中机械臂全身运动规划和安全关键控制框架，用于避障，并在仿真和硬件实验中展示了改进的性能。
summary_zh: 空中操作结合了多旋翼飞行器的高机动性和机械臂的灵巧性，从而能够在拥挤空间中执行复杂的任务。然而，由于全身碰撞避免以及常用几何抽象（如边界框或椭球体）的保守性，规划安全、动态可行的轨迹仍然很困难。我们提出了一种基于超二次曲面（SQ）的空中操作器全身运动规划和安全关键控制框架。使用 SQ 加代理表示，我们用可微分的、几何精度高的表面来建模飞行器和障碍物。利用这种表示，我们引入了一种最大间隙规划器，该规划器将 Voronoi 图与平衡流形公式融合，以生成平滑的、具有碰撞意识的轨迹。我们进一步设计了一个安全关键控制器，该控制器通过高阶控制障碍函数共同实施推力限制和碰撞避免。在仿真中，我们的方法在拥挤环境中优于基于采样的规划器，产生更快、更安全、更平滑的轨迹，并且在几何保真度方面超过了基于椭球体的基线。在真实的空中操作平台上进行的实际实验证实了可行性和鲁棒性，证明了在仿真和硬件设置中一致的性能。视频可在 https://youtu.be/hQYKwrWf1Ak 找到。

title: SuckTac: Camera-based Tactile Sucker for Unstructured Surface Perception and Interaction
summary: Suckers are significant for robots in picking, transferring, manipulation and locomotion on diverse surfaces. However, most of the existing suckers lack high-fidelity perceptual and tactile sensing, which impedes them from resolving the fine-grained geometric features and interaction status of the target surface. This limits their robust performance with irregular objects and in complex, unstructured environments. Inspired by the adaptive structure and high-performance sensory capabilities of cephalopod suckers, in this paper, we propose a novel, intelligent sucker, named SuckTac, that integrates a camera-based tactile sensor directly within its optimized structure to provide high-density perception and robust suction. Specifically, through joint structure design and optimization and based on a multi-material integrated casting technique, a camera and light source are embedded into the sucker, which enables in-situ, high-density perception of fine details like surface shape, texture and roughness. To further enhance robustness and adaptability, the sucker's mechanical design is also optimized by refining its profile, adding a compliant lip, and incorporating surface microstructure. Extensive experiments, including challenging tasks such as robotic cloth manipulation and soft mobile robot inspection, demonstrate the superior performance and broad applicability of the proposed system.
tldr: The paper introduces SuckTac, a novel sucker design integrating a camera-based tactile sensor for high-density surface perception and robust manipulation in unstructured environments.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种新型吸盘设计 SuckTac，它集成了基于相机的触觉传感器，用于在非结构化环境中进行高密度表面感知和鲁棒操作。
summary_zh: 吸盘在机器人抓取、转移、操作以及在各种表面上的移动中发挥着重要作用。然而，目前大多数吸盘都缺乏高保真度的感知和触觉传感能力，这阻碍了它们解析目标表面的精细几何特征和交互状态，从而限制了它们在处理不规则物体以及复杂、非结构化环境中的稳健性能。受头足类动物吸盘的自适应结构和高性能传感能力的启发，本文提出了一种新型的智能吸盘，命名为SuckTac，它将基于相机的触觉传感器直接集成到其优化结构中，以提供高密度感知和强劲吸力。具体而言，通过联合结构设计和优化，并基于多材料集成铸造技术，将相机和光源嵌入到吸盘中，从而能够对表面形状、纹理和粗糙度等精细细节进行原位、高密度感知。为了进一步提高稳健性和适应性，还通过优化吸盘轮廓、增加柔顺唇缘以及引入表面微结构来优化其机械设计。包括机器人布料操作和软体移动机器人检测等具有挑战性的任务在内的大量实验，证明了所提出系统的卓越性能和广泛适用性。

title: A Quantitative Comparison of Centralised and Distributed Reinforcement Learning-Based Control for Soft Robotic Arms
summary: This paper presents a quantitative comparison between centralised and distributed multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) architectures for controlling a soft robotic arm modelled as a Cosserat rod in simulation. Using PyElastica and the OpenAI Gym interface, we train both a global Proximal Policy Optimisation (PPO) controller and a Multi-Agent PPO (MAPPO) under identical budgets. Both approaches are based on the arm having $n$ number of controlled sections. The study systematically varies $n$ and evaluates the performance of the arm to reach a fixed target in three scenarios: default baseline condition, recovery from external disturbance, and adaptation to actuator failure. Quantitative metrics used for the evaluation are mean action magnitude, mean final distance, mean episode length, and success rate. The results show that there are no significant benefits of the distributed policy when the number of controlled sections $n\le4$. In very simple systems, when $n\le2$, the centralised policy outperforms the distributed one. When $n$ increases to $4< n\le 12$, the distributed policy shows a high sample efficiency. In these systems, distributed policy promotes a stronger success rate, resilience, and robustness under local observability and yields faster convergence given the same sample size. However, centralised policies achieve much higher time efficiency during training as it takes much less time to train the same size of samples. These findings highlight the trade-offs between centralised and distributed policy in reinforcement learning-based control for soft robotic systems and provide actionable design guidance for future sim-to-real transfer in soft rod-like manipulators.
tldr: This paper compares centralized and distributed reinforcement learning (RL) approaches for controlling soft robotic arms, finding trade-offs between sample efficiency, time efficiency, and robustness depending on the number of controlled sections.
tldr_zh: 本文比较了集中式和分布式强化学习（RL）方法在控制软体机器人手臂方面的应用，发现样本效率、时间效率和鲁棒性之间存在权衡，具体取决于控制部分的数量。
summary_zh: 本文对集中式和分布式多智能体强化学习(MARL)架构在控制软体机器人手臂(建模为模拟环境中的Cosserat杆)方面的性能进行了定量比较。利用PyElastica和OpenAI Gym接口，我们在相同的预算下训练了一个全局近端策略优化(PPO)控制器和一个多智能体PPO (MAPPO)。两种方法均基于手臂具有$n$个可控段落。该研究系统地改变$n$，并评估手臂在三种场景中到达固定目标的性能：默认基准条件、从外部扰动中恢复以及适应执行器故障。用于评估的定量指标包括平均动作幅度、平均最终距离、平均episode长度和成功率。结果表明，当可控段落数量$n\le4$时，分布式策略没有显著优势。在非常简单的系统，即$n\le2$时，集中式策略优于分布式策略。当$n$增加到$4< n\le 12$时，分布式策略表现出更高的样本效率。在这些系统中，分布式策略在局部可观测性下促进了更高的成功率、弹性和鲁棒性，并在相同样本量下产生更快的收敛速度。然而，集中式策略在训练期间实现了更高的时间效率，因为训练相同规模的样本花费的时间要少得多。这些发现突出了基于强化学习的软体机器人系统控制中集中式和分布式策略之间的权衡，并为软杆状机械臂中未来的仿真到现实迁移提供了可操作的设计指导。

title: Census-Based Population Autonomy For Distributed Robotic Teaming
summary: Collaborating teams of robots show promise due in their ability to complete missions more efficiently and with improved robustness, attributes that are particularly useful for systems operating in marine environments. A key issue is how to model, analyze, and design these multi-robot systems to realize the full benefits of collaboration, a challenging task since the domain of multi-robot autonomy encompasses both collective and individual behaviors. This paper introduces a layered model of multi-robot autonomy that uses the principle of census, or a weighted count of the inputs from neighbors, for collective decision-making about teaming, coupled with multi-objective behavior optimization for individual decision-making about actions. The census component is expressed as a nonlinear opinion dynamics model and the multi-objective behavior optimization is accomplished using interval programming. This model can be reduced to recover foundational algorithms in distributed optimization and control, while the full model enables new types of collective behaviors that are useful in real-world scenarios. To illustrate these points, a new method for distributed optimization of subgroup allocation is introduced where robots use a gradient descent algorithm to minimize portions of the cost functions that are locally known, while being influenced by the opinion states from neighbors to account for the unobserved costs. With this method the group can collectively use the information contained in the Hessian matrix of the total global cost. The utility of this model is experimentally validated in three categorically different experiments with fleets of autonomous surface vehicles: an adaptive sampling scenario, a high value unit protection scenario, and a competitive game of capture the flag.
tldr: This paper introduces a layered model for multi-robot autonomy using a census-based approach for collective decision-making and multi-objective behavior optimization for individual actions, validated experimentally with autonomous surface vehicles.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种多机器人自主性的分层模型，该模型采用基于人口普查的方法进行集体决策，并采用多目标行为优化进行个体行动决策，并通过自主水面车辆的实验验证。
summary_zh: 机器人协作团队因其能够更高效且更具鲁棒性地完成任务而展现出潜力，这些特性对于在海洋环境中运行的系统尤其有用。一个关键问题是如何建模、分析和设计这些多机器人系统，以充分实现协作优势，这是一项具有挑战性的任务，因为多机器人自主的领域涵盖了集体和个体行为。本文介绍了一种多机器人自主的分层模型，该模型采用“人口普查”原则，即对来自邻居的输入进行加权计数，用于关于组队的集体决策，并结合多目标行为优化，用于关于动作的个体决策。“人口普查”组件被表示为一个非线性意见动力学模型，而多目标行为优化则使用区间规划来实现。该模型可以被简化以恢复分布式优化和控制中的基础算法，而完整模型则能够实现适用于现实场景的新型集体行为。为了说明这些要点，本文介绍了一种用于子组分配的分布式优化新方法，其中机器人使用梯度下降算法来最小化局部已知的成本函数部分，同时受到来自邻居的意见状态的影响，从而考虑未观察到的成本。通过这种方法，团队可以集体利用包含在全局总成本的 Hessian 矩阵中的信息。该模型的效用在三个类别不同的自主水面车辆编队实验中得到了实验验证：自适应采样场景、高价值单元保护场景以及夺旗对抗游戏。

title: TACO: Trajectory-Aware Controller Optimization for Quadrotors
summary: Controller performance in quadrotor trajectory tracking depends heavily on parameter tuning, yet standard approaches often rely on fixed, manually tuned parameters that sacrifice task-specific performance. We present Trajectory-Aware Controller Optimization (TACO), a framework that adapts controller parameters online based on the upcoming reference trajectory and current quadrotor state. TACO employs a learned predictive model and a lightweight optimization scheme to optimize controller gains in real time with respect to a broad class of trajectories, and can also be used to adapt trajectories to improve dynamic feasibility while respecting smoothness constraints. To enable large-scale training, we also introduce a parallelized quadrotor simulator supporting fast data collection on diverse trajectories. Experiments on a variety of trajectory types show that TACO outperforms conventional, static parameter tuning while operating orders of magnitude faster than black-box optimization baselines, enabling practical real-time deployment on a physical quadrotor. Furthermore, we show that adapting trajectories using TACO significantly reduces the tracking error obtained by the quadrotor.
tldr: The paper introduces TACO, a framework for online optimization of quadrotor controller parameters based on upcoming trajectory, enabling real-time adaptation and improved tracking performance compared to static tuning, validated with a new parallelized simulator.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了TACO，一个基于即将到来的轨迹在线优化四旋翼控制器参数的框架，与静态调优相比，它能够实现实时自适应并提高跟踪性能，并通过一个新的并行化模拟器进行了验证。
summary_zh: 四旋翼飞行器轨迹跟踪中的控制器性能高度依赖于参数调整，然而，标准方法通常依赖于固定的、手动调整的参数，这牺牲了特定任务的性能。我们提出了轨迹感知控制器优化（TACO），该框架基于即将到来的参考轨迹和当前四旋翼飞行器的状态在线调整控制器参数。 TACO采用一种学习的预测模型和一个轻量级的优化方案，实时优化控制器增益，适用于广泛的轨迹类型，并且还可用于调整轨迹，以在尊重平滑性约束的同时提高动态可行性。为了实现大规模训练，我们还引入了一个并行化的四旋翼飞行器模拟器，支持在各种轨迹上快速数据收集。在各种轨迹类型上的实验表明，TACO优于传统的静态参数调整，同时比黑盒优化基线快几个数量级，从而能够在物理四旋翼飞行器上进行实际的实时部署。此外，我们展示了使用TACO调整轨迹可以显著减少四旋翼飞行器获得的跟踪误差。

title: Stein-based Optimization of Sampling Distributions in Model Predictive Path Integral Control
summary: This paper presents a novel method for Model Predictive Path Integral (MPPI) control that optimizes sample generation towards an optimal trajectory through Stein Variational Gradient Descent (SVGD). MPPI is traditionally reliant on randomly sampled trajectories, often by a Gaussian distribution. The result can lead to sample deprivation, under-representing the space of possible trajectories, and yield suboptimal results. Through introducing SVGD updates in between MPPI environment steps, we present Stein-Optimized Path-Integral Inference (SOPPI), an MPPI/SVGD algorithm that can dynamically update noise distributions at runtime to shape a more optimal representation without an excessive increase in computational requirements. We demonstrate the efficacy of our method systems ranging from a Cart-Pole to a two-dimensional bipedal walking task, indicating improved performance above standard MPPI across a range of hyper-parameters and demonstrate feasibility at lower particle counts. We discuss the applicability of this MPPI/SVGD method to higher degree-of-freedom systems, as well as its potential to new developments in state-of-the-art differentiable simulators.
tldr: The paper introduces Stein-Optimized Path-Integral Inference (SOPPI), a novel MPPI control method that uses Stein Variational Gradient Descent (SVGD) to dynamically optimize sample generation, achieving improved performance and sample efficiency on robotic control tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种新的MPPI控制方法，即Stein优化路径积分推理（SOPPI），它使用Stein变分梯度下降（SVGD）来动态优化样本生成，从而在机器人控制任务中实现了更高的性能和样本效率。
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种新型模型预测路径积分（MPPI）控制方法，该方法通过Stein变分梯度下降（SVGD）优化样本生成，使其趋向于最优轨迹。传统的MPPI依赖于随机采样轨迹，通常采用高斯分布。这可能导致样本匮乏，未能充分表示可能的轨迹空间，并产生次优结果。通过在MPPI环境步骤之间引入SVGD更新，我们提出了Stein优化路径积分推断（SOPPI），这是一种MPPI/SVGD算法，可以在运行时动态更新噪声分布，以塑造更优的表示，而不会过度增加计算需求。我们通过从倒立摆到二维双足步行任务等系统验证了该方法的有效性，表明在各种超参数下，其性能均优于标准MPPI，并证明了在较低粒子数下的可行性。我们讨论了这种MPPI/SVGD方法在高自由度系统中的适用性，以及其在最先进的可微仿真器中进行新开发的潜力。

title: CostBench: Evaluating Multi-Turn Cost-Optimal Planning and Adaptation in Dynamic Environments for LLM Tool-Use Agents
summary: Current evaluations of Large Language Model (LLM) agents primarily emphasize task completion, often overlooking resource efficiency and adaptability. This neglects a crucial capability: agents' ability to devise and adjust cost-optimal plans in response to changing environments. To bridge this gap, we introduce CostBench, a scalable, cost-centric benchmark designed to evaluate agents' economic reasoning and replanning abilities. Situated in the travel-planning domain, CostBench comprises tasks solvable via multiple sequences of atomic and composite tools with diverse, customizable costs. It also supports four types of dynamic blocking events, such as tool failures and cost changes, to simulate real-world unpredictability and necessitate agents to adapt in real time. Evaluating leading open-sourced and proprietary models on CostBench reveals a substantial gap in cost-aware planning: agents frequently fail to identify cost-optimal solutions in static settings, with even GPT-5 achieving less than 75% exact match rate on the hardest tasks, and performance further dropping by around 40% under dynamic conditions. By diagnosing these weaknesses, CostBench lays the groundwork for developing future agents that are both economically rational and robust.
tldr: The paper introduces CostBench, a benchmark for evaluating LLM agents' ability to perform cost-optimal planning and adapt to dynamic environments when using tools, revealing deficiencies in current models.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了CostBench，一个用于评估LLM智能体在使用工具时进行成本优化规划并适应动态环境能力的基准，揭示了当前模型的不足。
summary_zh: 目前对大型语言模型（LLM）代理的评估主要强调任务完成，往往忽略资源效率和适应性。这忽略了一项关键能力：代理根据变化的环境制定和调整成本最优计划的能力。为了弥补这一差距，我们引入了 CostBench，这是一个可扩展的、以成本为中心的基准，旨在评估代理的经济推理和重规划能力。CostBench 位于旅行规划领域，包含可通过具有多样化、可定制成本的原子和复合工具的多个序列解决的任务。它还支持四种类型的动态阻塞事件，例如工具故障和成本变化，以模拟现实世界中的不可预测性，并迫使代理实时地适应。在 CostBench 上评估领先的开源和专有模型揭示了成本感知规划方面的巨大差距：代理经常无法在静态设置中识别成本最优解决方案，即使是 GPT-5 在最困难的任务上也仅达到低于 75% 的精确匹配率，并且在动态条件下性能进一步下降约 40%。通过诊断这些弱点，CostBench 为开发未来兼具经济合理性和稳健性的代理奠定了基础。

title: Adaptive GR(1) Specification Repair for Liveness-Preserving Shielding in Reinforcement Learning
summary: Shielding is widely used to enforce safety in reinforcement learning (RL), ensuring that an agent's actions remain compliant with formal specifications. Classical shielding approaches, however, are often static, in the sense that they assume fixed logical specifications and hand-crafted abstractions. While these static shields provide safety under nominal assumptions, they fail to adapt when environment assumptions are violated. In this paper, we develop an adaptive shielding framework based on based on Generalized Reactivity of rank 1 (GR(1)) specifications, a tractable and expressive fragment of Linear Temporal Logic (LTL) that captures both safety and liveness properties. Our method detects environment assumption violations at runtime and employs Inductive Logic Programming (ILP) to automatically repair GR(1) specifications online, in a systematic and interpretable way. This ensures that the shield evolves gracefully, ensuring liveness is achievable and minimally weakening goals only when necessary. We consider two case studies: Minepump and Atari Seaquest; showing that (i) static symbolic controllers are often severely suboptimal when optimizing for auxiliary rewards, and (ii) RL agents equipped with our adaptive shield maintain near-optimal reward and perfect logical compliance compared with static shields.
tldr: The paper proposes an adaptive shielding framework for reinforcement learning using GR(1) specifications, which dynamically repairs specifications online to maintain safety and liveness when environment assumptions are violated, demonstrated in Minepump and Atari Seaquest environments.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一个基于GR(1)规范的强化学习自适应屏蔽框架，该框架能够在线动态修复规范，以便在环境假设被违反时保持安全性和活性。该方法在Minepump和 Atari Seaquest环境中进行了演示。
summary_zh: 屏蔽广泛应用于强化学习 (RL) 中以确保安全性，保证智能体的行为符合正式规范。然而，传统的屏蔽方法通常是静态的，即它们假定固定的逻辑规范和手工设计的抽象。虽然这些静态屏蔽在名义假设下提供安全性，但当环境假设被违反时，它们无法适应。在本文中，我们开发了一种基于广义反应性一级 (GR(1)) 规范的自适应屏蔽框架，GR(1) 是线性时序逻辑 (LTL) 的一个易于处理且富有表现力的片段，可同时捕获安全性和活性属性。我们的方法在运行时检测环境假设违规，并采用归纳逻辑编程 (ILP) 以系统且易于理解的方式在线自动修复 GR(1) 规范。这确保了屏蔽能优雅地演化，保证了活性能实现，并且仅在必要时最小程度地弱化目标。我们考虑了两个案例研究：矿井抽水和雅达利海战；表明 (i) 在优化辅助奖励时，静态符号控制器通常严重次优，并且 (ii) 与静态屏蔽相比，配备我们自适应屏蔽的 RL 智能体保持接近最优的奖励和完美的逻辑合规性。

title: SigmaCollab: An Application-Driven Dataset for Physically Situated Collaboration
summary: We introduce SigmaCollab, a dataset enabling research on physically situated human-AI collaboration. The dataset consists of a set of 85 sessions in which untrained participants were guided by a mixed-reality assistive AI agent in performing procedural tasks in the physical world. SigmaCollab includes a set of rich, multimodal data streams, such as the participant and system audio, egocentric camera views from the head-mounted device, depth maps, head, hand and gaze tracking information, as well as additional annotations performed post-hoc. While the dataset is relatively small in size (~ 14 hours), its application-driven and interactive nature brings to the fore novel research challenges for human-AI collaboration, and provides more realistic testing grounds for various AI models operating in this space. In future work, we plan to use the dataset to construct a set of benchmarks for physically situated collaboration in mixed-reality task assistive scenarios. SigmaCollab is available at https://github.com/microsoft/SigmaCollab.
